title: Print Three Lines in Order
prompt_md:
## Goal
Write a complete C++ program that prints three specific lines of text to standard output in the correct order.

## Input
None. This program takes no input.

## Output
Print exactly three lines:
```
First
Second
Third
```
(Each word on its own line, no trailing spaces, no blank lines between them.)

## Requirements
1. Use exactly three `std::cout` statements — one per line of output.
2. Your `main` function must return `0`.
3. Include `#include <iostream>` at the top of the file.

title: Fix the Broken Greeting
prompt_md:
## Goal
The program below is supposed to print a two-line greeting, but it contains one logic bug. Find and fix it so the output matches what is described.

## Input
None. This program takes no input.

## Output
The fixed program must print exactly:
```
Hello, C++!
I am ready to learn.
```

## Requirements
1. Do not add or remove any `std::cout` statements — only fix the existing code.
2. Your `main` function must return `0`.
3. The output must match the expected text exactly, including punctuation and capitalization.

test_cases.label: Sample: correct order
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Hello, C++!
I am ready to learn.

test_cases.label: Hidden: exact text match
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Hello, C++!
I am ready to learn.

test_cases.label: Hidden: no reversed order
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Hello, C++!
I am ready to learn.
